spec_match.rationale: The sync inspector claims to prove that <selectedcontent> mirrors update in the same task using microsecond-accurate timing. However, the mirrors are actually regular divs (#val-a, #val-b, #val-c) updated manually inside a synchronous JS loop. This always yields the same timestamp and proves absolutely nothing about the native browser implementation.

interactive.rationale: Users can pick colors and observe live microseconds log updates and a cumulative sync counter.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns with the concept of deferred simultaneous updates of multiple slot instances.

edge_cases.rationale: Uses performance.now() to showcase execution times but represents a synthetic synchronous test instead of real browser slot updates.

references.rationale: Cross-references multiple-mirrors, mutation-timing, and styled-mirrors correctly.

design_system.rationale: Clean monospace output formatting and clear badge status states.

openQuestions.title: Use real native elements for sync observation
openQuestions.detail: The demo should use actual native <selectedcontent> elements and a MutationObserver to observe the exact microtask ticks at which the browser updates them, rather than measuring a synchronous JS loop over standard divs.

summary: A simulated sync inspector that measures synchronous JS execution over standard divs rather than natively tracking actual <selectedcontent> microtask slots.
